data.title: Sweetgreen CFO Mitch Reback to retire; Jamie McConnell named successor
data.description:
• Sweetgreen (NYSE:SG) said on Thursday that Chief Financial Officer Mitch Reback will retire on September 21, 2025, and will be succeeded by Jamie McConnell, effective September 22, 2025.

  • Reback, who joined Sweetgreen in 2015, played a key role in building the company’s financial infrastructure and guiding it through its transition to a public company. He will remain in an advisory role for six months to support the leadership transition.
  • McConnell brings over 20 years of financial leadership experience, most recently serving as Chief Accounting and Administrative Officer at Chipotle.
  • She has also held senior roles at Aviation Capital Group, Rent-A-Center, Allergan, and Deloitte.
  • SG +0.22% after hours to $8.76
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Great Elm Capital prices public offering of $50 million of 7.75% notes due 2030
data.description:
• Great Elm Capital (GECC) announced on Thursday the pricing of its underwritten public offering of $50 million aggregate principal amount of its 7.75% notes due 2030, resulting in net proceeds of approximately $48.1 million after payment of underwriting discounts and commissions and estimated offering expenses payable by the Company.
  • The Notes will mature on December 31, 2030, and may be redeemed in whole or in part at any time or from time to time at the company’s option on or after December 31, 2027.
  • The company has also granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional $7.5 million aggregate principal amount of Notes to cover over-allotments, if any.
  • The closing of the transaction is subject to customary closing conditions, and the Notes are expected to be delivered on or about September 11, 2025.
  • The Notes are expected to be listed on The Nasdaq Global Market under the trading symbol “GECCG,” and to trade thereon within 30 days from the original issue date.
  • The company expects to use the net proceeds from the offering to redeem all of its outstanding 8.75% notes due 2028 and the remainder of the proceeds may be used to redeem or repurchase all or a portion of its outstanding 5.875% notes due 2026 and to repurchase all or a portion of its outstanding 8.50% notes due 2029 as well as for the repurchase of all or a portion of its outstanding 8.125% notes due 2029 and to repay all or a portion of any borrowings outstanding under its revolving credit facility or for general corporate purposes, including making investments consistent with its investment objectives.
  • GEG -7.64% after hours to $2.7706.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Mirion Technologies set to join S&P SmallCap 600
data.description:
• Mirion Technologies (NYSE:MIR) will replace GMS (NYSE:GMS) in the S&P SmallCap 600 effective before the opening of trading on Tuesday, September 9.
  • S&P 500 and 100 constituent Home Depot (HD) acquired GMS in a deal that was completed today.
  • MIR stock up over 6% in extended trading.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump signs Japan trade pact, capping tariffs at 15%
data.description:
President Donald Trump on Thursday signed an order to implement the U.S.-Japan trade deal reached in July.

The move closes months of back-and-forth with Japan on deal terms, locking in a maximum 15% tariff on most Japanese goods. U.S. 

Most goods shipped after Aug. 7 will face the confirmed 15% duty, according to the order.

Meanwhile, tariff breaks for aerospace and auto imports kick in a week from Thursday.

And, as part of the agreement, the U.S. will roll back some duties on aircraft and parts, along with generic drugs, ingredients and precursor chemicals, the White House said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Gold could hit $5,000 next year if Fed independence threatened, Goldman Sachs says
data.description:
The gold price is poised to reach $5,000/oz next year if the independence of the U.S. Federal Reserve is damaged and investors shift just 1% of their holdings from Treasurys into bullion, Goldman Sachs said Thursday.

"A scenario where Fed independence is damaged would likely lead to higher inflation, lower stock and long-dated bond prices, and an erosion of the dollar's reserve currency status," while gold is "a store of value that doesn't rely on international trust," Goldman analysts including Samantha Dart said in a note.

Goldman outlined a range of possible outcomes for the yellow metal, with a baseline forecast for a rise to $4,000/oz by mid-2026, a "tail-risk" scenario of $4,500/oz, and an estimate of nearly $5,000/oz if just 1% of the privately-owned U.S. Treasury market flows into gold.

"As a result, gold remains our highest-conviction long recommendation," Dart and her team wrote.

Analysts at J.P. Morgan also pointed to a potential erosion of Fed independence in its forecast for gold to potentially hit $4,250/oz by the end of 2026.

The recent surge in the gold price - up 6.5% in six straight days of gains - followed President Trump's move to oust Fed governor Lisa Cook and replace her with a rate cut supporter.

On Thursday, gold futures fell modestly as traders took profits following the rally that lifted the metal to new all-time highs, with the focus now shifting to the U.S. payrolls report for fresh hints on the Fed's policy path and setting the tone for this month's Fed meeting.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for September delivery closed -0.7% to $3,565.80/oz, still the metal's second highest settlement in history, and front-month Comex September silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished -1.5% to $40.911/oz, snapping a five-session winning streak but still its third-highest settlement this year.

data.title: Cerence files patent infringement suit against Apple
data.description:
• Cerence (NASDAQ:CRNC) announced on Thursday the filing of a patent infringement lawsuit against Apple (AAPL) in the United States District Court for the Western District of Texas.
  • The lawsuit against Apple covers certain Cerence technologies, including text input and recognition, and voice command monitoring, deployed in Apple’s products, said the company in its press statement. 
  • “Cerence plans to defend its IP rights to protect our significant R&D investments and technical leadership,” said Jennifer Salinas, Chief Administrative Officer & General Counsel, Cerence AI.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Amneal gets FDA nod for generic version of Risperdal Consta
data.description:
• Amneal Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:AMRX) has received approval from the U.S. FDA for its antipsychotic, risperidone extended-release injectable suspension, a generic version of Janssen’s (JNJ) Risperdal Consta.
  • The product is available in dosages of 12.5 mg, 25 mg, 37.5 mg, and 50 mg per vial.
  • The company plans to launch this product in the fourth quarter of 2025.

data.title: MediaAlpha buys back $32.9 mln in shares from Insignia Capital
data.description:
• MediaAlpha (NYSE:MAX) said on Thursday that it has repurchased 3.23 million shares of its Class A common stock for approximately $32.9 million in a privately negotiated deal with entities affiliated with Insignia Capital Group.
  • The repurchase price of $10.17 per share reflects a 5.5% discount to MediaAlpha’s closing stock price on September 2, 2025.
  • The deal, approved by a special committee of independent directors, involved Insignia exchanging an equal number of Class B shares and Class B-1 units in company's QL Holdings, LLC subsidiary for the Class A shares. The repurchased shares will be canceled and retired.
  • “Privately repurchasing these shares at a discount is immediately accretive," CFO Thompson, said in a statement.
  • MAX +1.92% after hours to $12.21.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Broadcom in charts: Semiconductor revenue rises 26% YY in Q3
